panel.title: 🧠 Armis Centrix Platform Overview
panel.html:
Armis Centrix™ provides cyber exposure management by discovering, classifying, and monitoring assets across IT, OT, IoT, and IoMT environments.

The Armis API allows external systems such as Splunk to retrieve asset intelligence, alerts, vulnerabilities, and operational data from the Armis platform.

This Splunk application uses those APIs to ingest Armis telemetry and present operational dashboards, analytics, and reporting.

panel.title: 🔐 API Authentication
panel.html:
All Armis API requests require authentication using a temporary access token.

The token is generated using the following endpoint: 

POST /api/v1/access_token/

Required parameter:

  • secret-key

The API returns:

  • access_token
  • expiration_utc

The token must be included in all API requests using the Authorization header.

panel.title: 🔎 Armis Search API (ASQ)
panel.html:
The primary API used to retrieve information from Armis is the Search API.

Searches are executed using Armis Standard Query (ASQ). 

GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:devices
GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:alerts
GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:activity

ASQ supports time filtering: 

in:alerts after:2022-03-11T14:00:00 before:2022-03-16T14:00:00

ASQ queries can be used to retrieve alerts, devices, activity, and other telemetry from the Armis platform.

panel.title: 🚨 Alerts API
panel.html:
Alerts represent security events or anomalies detected within the Armis environment. 

GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:alerts

Example alert fields include:

  • alertId
  • severity
  • status
  • time
  • title
  • type
  • deviceIds
  • connectionIds

Alerts may represent anomalous behavior, security violations, or suspicious activity detected by Armis.

panel.title: 💻 Devices API
panel.html:
Devices represent discovered assets in the environment including IT devices, OT equipment, IoT devices, and unmanaged systems. 

GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:devices

Device retrieval supports pagination parameters:

  • length — number of records returned
  • from — starting offset for pagination

The Armis API supports page sizes up to 5000 records.

panel.title: 📡 Activity Events
panel.html:
Activity events represent network or device behavior detected by Armis sensors and collectors. 

GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:activity

Example activity fields include:

  • activityUUID
  • title
  • content
  • protocol
  • deviceIds
  • connectionIds
  • sensor.name
  • time

panel.title: 🏷️ Device Tags
panel.html:
Device tags allow administrators to assign labels to assets for grouping and operational management. 

POST /api/v1/devices_tags/

Example parameters:

  • ips
  • macs
  • tags
  • remove

Tags can be applied or removed for sets of devices.

panel.title: 🔗 Integrations
panel.html:
Integrations connect the Armis platform with external systems such as SIEMs, ticketing systems, or enforcement platforms. 

GET /api/v2/integrations/

Integration records include:

  • id
  • instance
  • integrationState
  • creationTime
  • changeTime
  • collectorId

panel.title: 📄 Pagination
panel.html:
Large datasets must be retrieved using pagination parameters.

  • length — number of results per page
  • from — starting record offset

Example: 

GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:devices&length=100
GET /api/v1/search/?aql=in:devices&length=100&from=100

The response returns metadata including total record counts and the next pagination offset.

panel.title: ⚙️ API Best Practices
panel.html:
• Always authenticate using an access token.
  • Use pagination for large data sets.
  • Filter queries by time when possible.
  • Use ASQ queries to retrieve targeted datasets.
  • Validate API responses and error codes.
